Document:
# Baking
Baking often involves several mixing and preparatory steps before a final product can be made. Many such steps will involve baking in the oven or applying a topping onto a intermediate step.
Measurements here will be written in [color=cyan]units[/color]. Several recipes require small amounts of certain reagents, measuring spoons included in baking kits can aid in this process.
## Cakes
Cakes come in many forms but they all start with a base layer of delicious fluff which is made by baking their respective batters for 15 seconds in an oven.
All batters will require a thorough mixing after the ingredients are in the container of your choosing.
- Regular Batter : 15u Flour, 12u Egg, 5u Sugar, 5u Milk, and 1u Vanilla Extract - Chocolate Batter: 15u Flour, 12u Egg, 5u Sugar, 5u Milk, and 5u Cocoa Powder - Cotton Batter: 10u Fiber, 10u Water, and 5u Flour
## Frostings
What is a dessert without a bit of extra sugar on top. Several different frostings can be applied to a cake base to enrich its flavor.
- Vanilla Frosting: 5u Milk, 5u Sugar, 2u Vanilla Extract - Spacemans Frosting: 5u Milk, 5u Sugar, 10u Polypyrylium Oligomers
After baking the batter for 15 seconds, the cakes - while edible - will feel very bare and can use a touch-up. This can be done by adding a topping such as frosting to them to seal the deal.
- Vanilla Cake : Add vanilla frosting - Spacemans Cake : Add spacemans frosting - Cotton Cake : Add cotton
